explanation: The request asks to resolve important product and compatibility choices before implementation.
The request asks to settle ambiguous caching requirements before any coding begins.
The request is a pre-implementation requirements discussion for a non-trivial API change.
The user explicitly wants a rough idea converted into a phased implementation plan with validation.
The user asks for an overall engineering plan from an early feature idea.
The requested phased migration plan matches the planning workflow directly.
The request is to execute and verify tasks from an existing implementation plan.
The user wants an existing phase checklist refined into executable and verifiable work.
The request is to continue an approved plan item by item with checks and status updates.
The user explicitly asks to turn an approved PRD into ordered implementation issues.
The request is for dependency-ordered issue drafts derived from an implementation plan.
The user wants a finished plan decomposed into independently verifiable implementation issues.
The requirements are fixed and the request includes implementation, focused tests, checks, and review.
This is an ordinary behaviour-preserving refactor with regression tests and configured checks.
The cause and fix are agreed, so the task is focused implementation with a regression test.
The numerical solver change requires scientific tolerances, benchmark fixtures, and invariant verification.
The request is test-driven scientific implementation involving stochastic reproducibility and distribution checks.
The calculation is scientific code requiring explicit units, conservation checks, benchmarks, and tolerances.
The request does not say whether the user wants discovery, planning, implementation, or review.
The request combines planning and immediate implementation without saying whether requirements are settled.
The user wants an existing plan and phase checklist refined for executable verification.
The user asks to onboard a newly created repository before coding.
This is first-time repository onboarding without an existing handoff or diary.
The request is to establish a new project's context and workflow before implementation.
The user explicitly asks to resume a mature repository from its handoff and working diary.
The request is to restore established project context after compaction before continuing work.
The user asks to resume a mature repository by reconciling its handoff, diary, and Git state.
The requested evidence-backed single-page HTML project status report matches this skill exactly.
The user asks for an evidence-backed HTML project-health report and recommended next steps.
The request directly specifies a progressively detailed one-page project briefing.
The user is pausing work and wants durable continuity notes for another agent.
The request is to close the session with a structured handoff and exact next steps.
The user explicitly wants a safe handoff to a fresh Codex session.
The request gives no indication whether the project is new, continuing, being reviewed, or being changed.
The request spans status reporting and project continuation without identifying which workflow should lead.
The user wants an orientation to an unfamiliar repository's structure and runtime flows before editing.
The request is to explain control flow, data flow, and relevant modules in an unfamiliar service.
The user asks for a technical tour of an existing codebase and its change hotspots.
The request explicitly targets architectural ownership, boundaries, coupling, duplication, and improvements.
The user wants an architecture assessment of scalability, dependency risks, and refactoring opportunities.
The task is to identify architectural duplication and misplaced responsibilities across a repository.
The user explicitly requests an interactive HTML explanation of a pull request with diagrams and a quiz.
The requested self-contained web explanation of a commit matches the rich diff explainer workflow.
The user asks for an interactive HTML lesson explaining a branch comparison.
The request is a findings-led diff review for bugs, regressions, tests, and unsafe edge cases.
The user wants a pull request reviewed for breakage risks, test sufficiency, and required fixes.
The request is a severity-ordered review of current working-tree changes.
The user asks for evidence-led diagnosis and reproduction without a speculative fix.
The request is to investigate a surprising production output and establish its actual cause before changes.
The user wants a regression isolated with a minimal reproduction before implementation is edited.
The request does not say whether the changes need a correctness review, an explanation, or an architecture assessment.
The user asks to create a new reusable RB-style agent skill and its supporting metadata.
The request is to improve an existing skill's triggers, instructions, and metadata.
The user wants a complete RB-style skill with instructions, metadata, validation, and references.
The request is to build repeated behavioural routing evaluations for an agent skill.
The user explicitly asks for matched skill and no-skill trials with blinded scoring.
The request is to add regression evaluation coverage for an over-triggering agent skill.
The user asks to synchronize versioned skill folders into Codex through symlinks.
The request concerns publishing and synchronizing a skills repository across computers.
The user wants selected repository skills copied into a Claude Code skills directory.
The request is to diagnose and repair stale global skill links and project discovery resources.
The global skills already exist and the task is to repair incomplete project resources and discovery checks.
The user wants an audit and repair of installed-skill and project-resource discovery configuration.
The request covers full skill installation, project resources, visibility checks, and onboarding on a new machine.
The user asks for complete RB workflow setup followed by initial project onboarding.
The requested end-to-end setup matches the full installation and verification workflow.
The request does not identify whether skills must be authored, evaluated, synchronized, installed, or repaired.
The user explicitly asks to instantiate and configure a new LLM wiki from the template.
The request is to create and validate a fresh LLM wiki from wiki-template.
The user wants a brand-new agent wiki configured from the template and verified.
The request directly describes processing an existing LLM wiki inbox and validating ingested sources.
The user asks to run the supported ingestion workflow for papers placed in a wiki inbox.
The request covers source registration, reference-page creation, quick validation, and ingestion reporting.
The user explicitly asks for supported nightly LLM wiki maintenance and health reporting.
The request is a non-destructive wiki health and quick-maintenance workflow.
The user asks for the established wiki's weekly deep-clean and maintenance report.
The request does not say whether the wiki should be ingested, maintained, queried, or substantively edited.
The user explicitly requests a durable working diary for a multi-session investigation.
The request is to preserve durable task context across chat compaction.
The user wants compact durable notes throughout a long debugging and planning effort.
The task is to create shared project vocabulary and update CONTEXT.md with domain meaning and assumptions.
The request is to document scientific variables, units, ranges, invariants, and terminology in project context.
The user wants an authoritative project vocabulary section in CONTEXT.md.
The explicit token command directly triggers the context-token reporting skill.
The user asks for latest-call input tokens and current context-window usage.
The request directly asks for the active-session token count from Codex token events.
The user asks to design agent boundaries, orchestration, state, evaluation, and failure containment.
The request is to debug multi-agent orchestration, handoffs, termination, traces, and recovery.
The user wants architectural choices among deterministic tools, embedded capabilities, specialist agents, and orchestration.
The user wants novelty and prior-art evaluation with falsifiable hypotheses before planning or coding.
The request is to gate a scientific hypothesis through literature threats, falsifiers, and a proceed decision.
The user explicitly asks for a prior-art and novelty gate before building anything.
The requirements are agreed and the task is an ordinary product implementation with focused tests.
The request is to settle requirements for one model call before implementation, with no multi-agent system involved.
The user asks for a phased implementation plan for a single-assistant product rather than multi-agent design.
The request does not say whether the needed artifact is a glossary, working diary, handoff, status report, or codebase explanation.
